Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: Microsoft Research (developed at Microsoft AI & Research); original team led by Shital Shah, Debadeepta Dey, and Ashish Kapoor

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source simulator for autonomous vehicles (drones/quadrotors and ground vehicles) built on Unreal Engine; supports testing AI systems, computer vision, deep learning, and reinforcement learning for autonomous vehicle research; provides photorealistic environments, physics simulation, and sensor models (cameras, LiDAR, IMU)

Response: C++, Python, other (Unreal Engine Blueprint scripting)

Metric: Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: AirSim was built on Unreal Engine 4 and also provides a Unity plugin. Microsoft officially archived the repository in November 2022, citing resource constraints. A successor, Microsoft Project AirSim, is under development as a commercial product. Community forks (e.g., Colosseum by CodexLabsLLC) continue active development. v1.8.1 was the final official release.

Metric: Overall impression? (1 .. 10)

Section: Installability (Measured via installation on a virtual machine.)
Metric: Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Installation is complex due to the Unreal Engine dependency. Setting up Unreal Engine on Linux from source is a multi-hour process. Using prebuilt environment binaries significantly reduces effort. The airsim Python client installs easily via pip. The Unreal Engine requirement is a significant barrier to entry.

Response: The hello_drone.py and hello_car.py examples worked correctly with the prebuilt environments. Photorealistic rendering quality matched documentation screenshots. No CI updates since archival in 2022.

Response: Installation and initial simulation ran reliably. Unreal Engine provides a stable rendering foundation. The archived status means bugs discovered after November 2022 will not receive official fixes.

Response: Python API error messages are informative. Connection timeout and server unavailability produce clear error messages. JSON configuration file parsing is tolerant of minor formatting variations.

Response: GitHub Issues (archived — read only), AirSim Discord server, Stack Overflow (airsim tag), documentation at microsoft.github.io/AirSim, community forks (Colosseum) for active support

Metric: Overall impression? (1 .. 10)

Response: Documentation was comprehensive at time of archival. The GitHub Issues are now read-only. Community support has migrated to Discord and the Colosseum fork. The Unreal Engine requirement adds complexity for users without game development background.

Response: Multi-language API support (Python, C++, ROS, MATLAB, Java). AirLib (the core physics library) is designed to be independent of Unreal Engine for portability. Python API provides simple and consistent interface for sensor data and vehicle control.
